drugs.adult_standard_dose: 500 mg every 8 h or 875 mg every 12 h (oral)
drugs.adult_max_dose: 3000 mg/day (standard); up to 4000 mg/day for severe infections

weight_based.notes: Weight-based dosing primarily used in paediatrics; adult cap at 500 mg/dose unless high-dose regimen

renal_adjustment.esrd_dialysis: 500 mg every 24 h; supplement after haemodialysis

hepatic_adjustment.severe: Use with caution; no specific guidance

drugs.adult_max_dose: 60 mg/kg/day (loading dose up to 35 mg/kg in critically ill)

weight_based.notes: Use actual body weight (ABW) in most patients; consult pharmacy for obese patients (>BMI 40). Dose by AUC/MIC monitoring per 2020 ASHP/IDSA guidelines

renal_adjustment.mild_impairment: Extend interval to every 12 h; monitor levels
renal_adjustment.moderate_impairment: Extend interval to every 24–48 h; target trough <10 mg/L
renal_adjustment.severe_impairment: Dose by level monitoring only; may need once-weekly dosing
renal_adjustment.esrd_dialysis: Load 25 mg/kg; re-dose based on post-dialysis levels

drugs.pediatric_notes: 40–60 mg/kg/day divided every 6 h IV; neonates require specialised protocols

drugs.adult_standard_dose: 500 mg twice daily or 850 mg once daily with meals; titrate over 2–4 weeks

renal_adjustment.moderate_impairment: Contraindicated if eGFR <45 at initiation; discontinue if eGFR falls <30

hepatic_adjustment.mild: Use with caution — hepatic disease associated with lactic acidosis risk

drugs.pediatric_notes: ≥10 years: 500 mg twice daily; max 2000 mg/day

drugs.adult_standard_dose: 10 mg once daily (hypertension); 5 mg once daily (heart failure, start low)

renal_adjustment.esrd_dialysis: Start at 2.5 mg/day; dialysable — supplement post-dialysis

drugs.pediatric_notes: 6–16 years: 0.07 mg/kg once daily (max 5 mg/day); titrate to 0.61 mg/kg/day

drugs.adult_standard_dose: Individualised by INR target (2.0–3.0 for most indications)
drugs.adult_max_dose: No fixed maximum — dose to INR target

renal_adjustment.mild_impairment: No direct dose change; monitor INR closely
renal_adjustment.moderate_impairment: No direct dose change; monitor INR closely
renal_adjustment.severe_impairment: No direct dose change; monitor INR closely — uremia alters protein binding

hepatic_adjustment.mild: Reduce dose; liver synthesises clotting factors — impaired clearance
hepatic_adjustment.moderate: Reduce dose significantly; INR may be unreliable marker

drugs.pediatric_notes: Initiate at 0.2 mg/kg/day; target INR 2.0–3.0; requires specialist management

drugs.adult_standard_dose: 25–50 mg every 4–6 h orally (antihistamine/allergy); 50 mg at bedtime (sleep aid)
drugs.adult_max_dose: 300 mg/day (antihistamine); 50 mg/dose as sleep aid — do not exceed

weight_based.notes: Weight-based dosing used in paediatrics only (≥2 years). Adult standard fixed dose (25–50 mg) is preferred for adults — do not exceed 1.5 mg/kg per dose or 5 mg/kg/day in children. OTC Benadryl tablet (25 mg) = standard adult dose; two tablets (50 mg) = maximum single adult dose.

renal_adjustment.mild_impairment: Use with caution; no specific dose reduction required
renal_adjustment.moderate_impairment: Extend dosing interval to every 6–12 h; monitor for anticholinergic toxicity
renal_adjustment.severe_impairment: Extend dosing interval to every 12–18 h; accumulation risk
renal_adjustment.esrd_dialysis: Administer every 12–18 h; not significantly dialysed

hepatic_adjustment.moderate: Reduce dose or extend interval; hepatic metabolism impaired
hepatic_adjustment.severe: Avoid if possible; significant accumulation risk

drugs.pediatric_notes: ≥2 years: 1–1.5 mg/kg/dose every 4–6 h (max 1.5 mg/kg/dose, max 5 mg/kg/day). NOT for use in children <2 years — risk of paradoxical CNS excitation and respiratory depression. For the Benadryl 25 mg tablet: children <12 years should use liquid formulation with weight-based dosing.
